methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
IQM Quantum Computers is a pre-IPO stage quantum computing company that has raised €600M+ to date. The quantum computing sector comprises a narrow universe of 11 comparable companies—3 public (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private firms including Quantinuum and Infleqtion.

The peer-median valuation method is not applicable here. Despite identifying 7 same-sector peers, the quantum computing landscape lacks sufficient depth at IQM's stage and valuation tier. Zero same-stage comparables, zero adjacent-stage peers, and zero similar-tier private companies meet the minimum threshold of three datapoints required for statistical reliability. Applying an all-sector median would obscure rather than clarify valuation, given the heterogeneity of business models, technology approaches, and commercialization timelines across the quantum sector.

This analysis therefore cannot triangulate a defensible valuation range using conventional peer multiples. Any valuation of IQM requires alternative methods—such as discounted cash flow analysis (if revenue or path-to-profitability data exists), precedent transaction analysis within quantum computing, or venture capital method frameworks calibrated to quantum-sector risk profiles and exit horizons. The fundamental constraint is structural: the pre-IPO quantum computing segment is too sparse and early-stage to support multiple-based valuation. Key uncertainties center on which technology modalities (superconducting, ion trap, neutral atom) will achieve commercial traction, timeline to meaningful revenue, and capital requirements through maturity.
